title: SpaceX IPO Draws More Than $70 Billion in Retail Orders
summary: SpaceX’s initial public offering has attracted more than $70 billion in orders from retail investors, according to people familiar with the matter, as the potentially record-breaking debut enters the home stretch.

title: Tech Disruptors: Apollo on Funding the AI Infrastructure Boom
summary: “It’s clear to us that the world is short compute right now, and the industry is racing to catch up,” Rob Bittencourt, partner and head of thematic investing at Apollo, tells Bloomberg Intelligence’s Alexandra Davidov and Paul Gulberg on this episode of the Tech Disruptors podcast. Bittencourt discusses why AI is becoming a private-credit story, with trillions of dollars of data-center, power, chip and infrastructure investment needed to support the next phase of adoption. title: Vanguards of Health Care: Restor3D Personalizes Orthopedics
summary: “If you plan, the cases can go much more smoothly. And if you have patient-specific instrumentation, they can go much more quickly” says restor3D’s CEO Kurt Jacobus in this episode of Vanguards of Healthcare. title: Heat Pumps and the Economics of Clean Heating (Podcast)
summary: Heat pumps are at a crossroads. After a decade of growth, global sales fell in 2025 as weaker construction activity weighed on key markets. Yet the longer-term picture remains resilient. Heat pumps have continued to gain ground against fossil fuel heating in several regions, while Europe remains a focal point for adoption, shaped by a shifting mix of subsidies, energy prices and consumer demand. title: Drahi Is Squeezing His US Creditors Harder Than Ever
summary: If Patrick Drahi’s recent restructuring of his French telecoms business showed how far certain borrowers can squeeze their lenders, just look at what he is doing on the other side of the pond. The tycoon has pushed holders of $22 billion of debt in another of his companies into an extremely tight corner and once again the fine print may be on his side.

title: ECB Hikes Rates, Lifts Inflation Forecast Due to Iran War
US PPI Climbs at Fastest Pace in More Than Three Years
summary: US producer prices increased 6.5% in May from a year earlier, the fastest pace since November 2022, and advanced 1.1% from April as the fallout from the Iran war continued to fan inflation pressures. Michael McKee breaks down the data on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Abu Dhabi’s ADIC Plans $15 Billion Leveraged Hedge Fund Bet
summary: An Abu Dhabi-based investor is considering building exposure worth $15 billion with global hedge funds, people familiar with the matter said, an outlay that will mark one of the emirate’s most significant forays into the sector.

title: Stocks Pare Gains on US-Iran Jitters, PPI Report: Markets Wrap
summary: A Wall Street rebound waned as oil rose on concern about an escalation of the war between the US and Iran that will keep energy costs elevated, fueling inflation risks.

title: Odd Lots Is Taking Over Hong Kong (Or the Newsletter, At Least)
summary: China markets, bowls of noodles, and more.

title: Oil Turns Higher as US Threatens to Take Key Iranian Export Hub
summary: Oil gained after President Trump said the US would keep attacking Iran, while also threatening to seize the Kharg Island oil terminal that is vital for Tehran’s exports.

title: Amazon’s Record Bond Deal Shakes Up Canada’s Credit Market
summary: Amazon.com Inc.’s record loonie bond sale has the Canadian fixed income market working hard to accommodate the deluge of debt, pushing risk spreads higher and spurring other borrowers to delay note sales of their own.

title: ECB Hikes for First Time Since 2023 as Inflation Heats Up
summary: The European Central Bank raised interest rates for the first time in almost three years, concluding it can no longer wait out the Iran war as inflation pressures intensify.

title: Citigroup Offers Its First Investment-Grade Bonds of 2026
summary: Citigroup Inc. is offering its first investment-grade bonds of 2026, following $123.3 billion of issuance by the five other biggest Wall Street banks this year.

title: Air India 171 Crash Report Delayed
summary: One year after the crash of Air India 171, investigators are still searching for definitive answers. The final report has been delayed as probes continue into the aircraft's engines and systems. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Iran’s Oil Production Slumped 19% Last Month, OPEC Data Show
summary: Iran’s crude oil production slumped by 19% last month, according to data from OPEC, while the US blockaded the country’s ports during their ongoing conflict.

title: US Nuclear Power’s Technological ‘Rebirth’ Will Take Some Time
summary: The first small-reactor startup in a government program reaches the milestone of a self-sustaining reaction.

title: Base10, Black-Led VC Firm, Boosts Managed Assets to $2.6 Billion
summary: Base10 Partners, one of the biggest Black-led venture capital firms, has closed two funds and boosted assets under management to $2.6 billion.

title: Retail Traders Dump Big Tech to Raise ‘Dry Powder’ to Buy SpaceX
summary: Retail traders’ infatuation with high-flying computer memory and storage companies is fading now that the next big thing — SpaceX’s initial public offering — is on the way.

title: Data Center Battery Maker ZincFive to Merge With SparkLabs SPAC
summary: ZincFive Inc., a specialized battery maker supplying data centers, has agreed to go public through a merger with a blank-check company backed by SparkLabs Group at a pre-money valuation of $600 million.

title: Software Stock Reversal Has Traders Bracing for More Pain Ahead
summary: A recent rally in software stocks is proving to be short-lived, and investors are bracing for more selling as fears linger about artificial intelligence disruption.

title: Lloyds Plans SRT Tied to $4.2 Billion of Loans to Smaller Firms
summary: Lloyds Banking Group Plc is looking to sell its first significant risk transfer deal of the year, linked to loans to small and medium-sized companies, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Fresh US Strikes On Iran Risk Fragile Ceasefire | The Opening Trade 6/11/2026
summary:
The second straight day after President Trump accused the country of dragging out talks on an interim peace deal. The attacks followed strikes on Tuesday in retaliation for the downing of a US Apache helicopter.

The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Anna Edwards and Guy Johnson. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Stocks Rebound; Oracle Falls on Spending Concerns; SpaceX IPO Pricing | Bloomberg Brief 6/11/2026
summary: Traders buy the dip. US stocks bounce back from a five-week low ahead of the SpaceX IPO pricing. Oracle drops as data center spending overshadows AI growth. Marvin Loh of State Street discusses the Fed's rate path. Monti Saroya, co-head of the flagship fund at Vista Equity Partners joins Bloomberg's Dani Burger at SuperReturn in Berlin.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ‘We’re Traders, Not Nuns’: Embattled Brazil Hedge-Fund Plots Turnaround
summary: Rogerio Xavier wants to set some things straight.

title: Dangote Refinery Seeks to Raise $1 Billion in Private Debt Sale
summary: Nigerian tycoon Aliko Dangote’s refinery unit is seeking to raise as much as $1 billion through a private sale of debt, people with knowledge of the plan said.

title: Heart Health Biotech Kardigan Seeks $373.3 Million in US IPO
summary: Kardigan Inc. is seeking to raise $373.3 million in a US initial public offering to help fund three late-stage drugs that target cardiovascular diseases.

title: SpaceX IPO a Datapoint to Higher Market Prices, Says Julian Emanuel
summary: Julian Emanuel, chief equity and quantitative strategist at Evercore ISI, says the melt up has begun as markets remain resilient ahead of the SpaceX IPO “landmark moment.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Takaichi Says 100% of Japan’s Oil Supply to Avoid Hormuz in July
summary: Japan’s crude oil imports in July are expected to be fully sourced from areas that don’t require transit through the Strait of Hormuz, Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said Thursday, as she seeks to diversify the resource-poor nation’s energy supply ahead of the hot summer months.

title: Goldman’s Hammond Says Analysts Underestimate AI Spending Boom
summary: Analysts are too conservative in their outlook for artificial intelligence spending next year, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc. strategists, who expect further gains for stocks linked to the theme.

title: Wall Street’s Worries Are All About AI
summary: Across bonds, credit and stocks, big names are putting their worries on record.

title: ChinaAMC on North Asia Allocations
summary: Yimei Li, CEO, China Asset Management Company tells Bloomberg’s Minmin Low interest in China onshore markets from international investors is still more on the transactional side, with long time allocators not fully back yet. Li was speaking at Bloomberg Invest 2026 in Hong Kong.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Norrsken's Freimane on Bloomberg TV
summary: Norrsken VC General Partner Agate Freimane speaks with Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie from the Founders Forum in Oxfordshire, UK. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Fundamental CEO Fraenkel on Bloomberg TV
summary: Fundamental CEO and Co-Founder Jeremy Fraenkel speaks with Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie from the Founders Forum in Oxfordshire, UK. (Source: Bloomberg)
